rationale: Ground truth is a clean 'lgtm' approve with zero substantive concerns, and X reaches the same conclusion with grounded verification (upstream match, no leftover references, existing tests updated) without inventing issues. Y's mechanical hunk-sweep is accurate but then manufactures two low-value nits (an out-of-scope test suggestion for an untouched code path, and a comment suggestion that contradicts the PR's explicit purpose of matching upstream naming), which are actionable but not genuinely valid concerns for this trivial rename fix. Neither has a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
